join_order.notes: custkeys dense 1..1500000, bitset ~187KB fits in cache
use lineitem_orderkey_index for contiguous range lookups

data_structures.aggregation: thread_local_compact_hash_map(capacity=32768_slots, key=int32_t(l_orderkey), value=struct{double revenue, int32_t o_orderdate, int32_t o_shippriority})

parallelism.merge: NO MERGE NEEDED: orders is sorted by o_orderkey (= GROUP BY key), so morsel-driven scan produces disjoint orderkey ranges per thread. Each thread's map contains complete, non-overlapping groups. Extract local top-10 from each thread map independently (parallelizable), then merge 64 local top-10 lists into global top-10.

indexes.purpose: not needed — build bitset directly from c_custkey + c_mktsegment arrays
contiguous range lookup for lineitem rows per qualifying orderkey

optimization_notes: Category A — Plan restructuring. Root cause: the merge phase (89ms, 46% of total) was sequentially merging 64 thread-local hash maps into one, then scanning for top-10. This is completely unnecessary because orders is sorted by o_orderkey (the GROUP BY key), so morsel-driven scan naturally partitions orderkeys across threads — each thread's map is already disjoint. Fix: (1) eliminate the merge entirely — each thread extracts its own local top-10 from its map, then merge 64×10=640 candidates into the global top-10; (2) right-size thread-local maps from 256K slots (6.3MB each, 403MB total — 9× LLC) down to 32K slots (768KB each, 48MB total — fits near LLC), since each thread only has ~15K groups; (3) the scan+probe phase should also benefit from reduced cache pressure on the aggregation maps. Expected improvement: merge drops from 89ms to <1ms, scan drops from 55ms to ~35ms due to better cache utilization.
